highlights.titleZh: A new, inexpensive Chinese AI model is catching up with Anthropic, OpenAI on their home turf - Reuters
highlights.content: [Skip to main content](#main-content) # A new, inexpensive Chinese AI model is catching up with Anthropic, OpenAI on their home turf By [Laurie Chen](/authors/laurie-chen/) and [Aditya Soni](/authors/aditya-soni/) Item 1 of 2 OpenAI CEO Sam Altman attends an event in Tokyo, Japan February 3, 2025. REUTERS/Kim Kyung-Hoon/File Photo **[1/2]**OpenAI CEO Sam Altman attends an event in Tokyo, Japan February 3, 2025. REUTERS/Kim Kyung-Hoon/File Photo [Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab](https://www.reutersconnect.com/item/anthropic-v-openai-behind-the-bitter-battle-for-the-future-of-ai/dGFnOnJldXRlcnMuY29tLDIwMjU6bmV3c21sX1JDMlVNQ0E0QzNaMQ%3D%3D/?utm_medium=rcom-article-media&utm_campaign=rcom-rcp-lead) * Summary * Companies * There is growing interest from Western startups in switching to cheaper Chinese AI * Z.ai's GLM-5.2 performs nearly as well as Claude Opus 4.8 * Western enterprise adoption complicated by data security concerns BEIJING/BENGALURU, July 2 (Reuters) - Since DeepSeek shocked markets early last year with its cheap but powerful AI model, global consumers have been faced with a choice: Chinese offerings with lower prices and less capability or OpenAI or Anthropic, which have poured ​billions into development. A model called [GLM-5.2](https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/after-anthropic-shutdown-chinas-zai-closes-frontier-gap-it-plans-dual-listing-2026-06-25/), launched last month by Beijing-based startup Z.ai, may finally be closing that gap in terms of Western interest. Make sense of global markets with the Trading Day newsletter. Sign up [here.](/newsletters/trading-day/?location=article-paragraph&redirectUrl=%2Fworld%2Fchina%2Fa-new-inexpensive-chinese-ai-model-is-catching-up-with-anthropic-openai-their-2026-07-02%2F) GLM-5.2 has Silicon Valley buzzing with its coding and ‌agent capabilities, or the ability to execute complex tasks with minimal prompting, that almost rival leading U.S. offerings at a fraction of the cost, in what some experts are calling a "mini DeepSeek moment." It has quickly climbed the usage charts on third-party AI developer platforms like OpenRouter, where it now ranks above Anthropic's models, while executives from cloud data platform Snowflake's CEO Sridhar Ramaswamy to venture capitalist Marc Andreessen have lauded its abilities. "We now have a Chinese open-weight model that is as good as the currently available models ​from OpenAI and Anthropic," said David Sacks, U.S. President Donald Trump's former AI czar, last week before Washington lifted curbs on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models on Tuesday. Those capabilities have put Z.ai's GLM-5.2 model ​at the heart of a growing debate about whether China is finally catching up to the U.S. in the AI race, as technology executives warn that Washington's unpredictable ⁠regulation of the industry risks hampering its lead in the frontier technology. "It is just a tick below Opus 4.8 (from Anthropic) and right up there with GPT 5.5 (from OpenAI)," Sacks said of GLM-5.2 on the All-In podcast, adding ​that "we cannot afford to do things that slow our companies down." The Anthropic curbs and the [delayed](https://www.reuters.com/legal/litigation/openai-defers-public-rollout-gpt56-us-seeks-early-access-frontier-ai-models-2026-06-26/) public rollout of OpenAI's latest GPT-5.6 model have fueled global demand for the Chinese model, some experts said. "The international developer community is increasingly ​aware that relying solely on proprietary, U.S.-based API models carries significant risk," said Brian Tse, founder and CEO of Concordia AI, a Beijing-based consultancy focused on AI safety. GLM-5.2's positive global reception also suggests increased interest in cheaper open-source development because businesses are getting stung by the rising and often unpredictable costs of using AI to complete tasks, as closed-source agentic AI tools consume more tokens, the units used to measure AI usage. Z.ai, also known as Zhipu AI, declined to comment....

highlights.title: New AI Agents Pose ‘Existential Threat’ to How Grants Are Awarded - Inside Higher Ed
highlights.titleZh: New AI Agents Pose ‘Existential Threat’ to How Grants Are Awarded - Inside Higher Ed
highlights.content: [News](/news) * [Faculty](/news/faculty) * [Research](/news/faculty/research) July 02, 2026 # New AI Agents Pose ‘Existential Threat’ to How Grants Are Awarded The rapid development of technology is outpacing any attempts to reform assessment systems, researchers warn. By  [Seher Asaf for Times Higher Education](/author/seher-asaf-times-higher-education) The rise of more sophisticated artificial intelligence agents poses an “existential threat” to the way research funding is awarded, experts have warned, saying autonomous AI systems could further flood grant competitions with applications, making it harder to identify the best ideas. Speaking during a webinar organized by the League of European Research Universities, Geraint Rees, vice provost for research, innovation and global engagement at UCL, said a new wave of AI tools represents a fundamental shift from today’s widely used generative AI. Unlike large language models such as ChatGPT and Claude, AI agents can autonomously gather information, make decisions and produce work with minimal human oversight. #### Most Popular * [ED Issues New List of Professional Degrees After Court Order](https://www.insidehighered.com/news/quick-takes/2026/06/30/ed-issues-new-list-professional-degrees-after-court-order) * [U Tennessee to Pay Millions to Prof Fired for Kirk Comments](https://www.insidehighered.com/news/quick-takes/2026/07/01/u-tennessee-pay-millions-prof-fired-kirk-comments) * [Free Tuition for Adult Community College Learners](https://www.insidehighered.com/news/student-success/academic-life/2026/06/29/free-tuition-adult-community-college-learners) “In the context of grants, generative AI may help you polish or write a better application,” Rees said. “But agentic AI will go off and write the application and submit it for you.” Such systems can be trained on a researcher’s published work, funding criteria and previously successful grant applications to generate, review and improve proposals. “The marginal cost of producing an application falls to zero,” Rees said. “That’s important because it changes the nature of the problem. It’s not about polishing your grant. It’s about a system that’s designed fundamentally to assess human judgment and human ideas being fed outputs that simulate human judgment.” [Advertisement](https://solutions.insidehighered.com/branding) Rees and James Wilsdon, a professor of research policy at UCL and executive director of the Research on Research Institute, said AI agents are creating three major challenges for research funders—a massive increase in applications, “quality compression” and “convergence.” The pair’s [recent research](https://www.timeshighereducation.com/news/research-funders-flooded-ai-assisted-applications) analyzed data from 12 funders across seven research systems and found grant application volumes had increased by 57 percent between the launch of ChatGPT in 2022 and the end of 2025. Early data for 2026 suggests the rate of growth is accelerating even more, Wilsdon said. The second challenge is what the researchers described as “quality compression.” [Advertisement](https://solutions.insidehighered.com/branding) “When everyone’s proposal has optimization and great writing, the quality floor rises, but the ceiling stays the same,” Rees said. “It becomes harder to discriminate between really excellent ideas and ones that are just OK.” The longer-term concern is convergence, where AI systems generate grant applications while also assisting reviewers in assessing them. “If you’ve got agents writing, and agents reviewing, and the agents are talking to each other, they’re all trained on exactly the same work,” Rees said. “Over time, the system isn’t going to evaluate great ideas. It’s just going to measure how well agents simulate what funders have previously rewarded. That’s a real existential threat to what we’re trying to do.” #### Editor's Picks * [The Researcher Who Spent Years Studying Grass for the World Cup](/news/faculty/researc...

highlights.content: * [Skip to primary navigation](#genesis-nav-primary) * [Skip to primary sidebar](#genesis-sidebar-primary) * [Skip to secondary sidebar](#genesis-sidebar-secondary) [Robotics & Automation News](https://roboticsandautomationnews.com/) Where Innovation Meets Imagination # Top 7 AI Agent Platforms for Industrial Manufacturing in 2026 by [Sam Francis](https://roboticsandautomationnews.com/author/samfrancis/) **Manufacturing has entered a new phase of digital transformation. Over the past decade, factories invested heavily in IoT sensors, Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES), industrial analytics, and predictive maintenance solutions.** These technologies generated unprecedented operational visibility, allowing manufacturers to monitor equipment, production lines, quality metrics, and material flows in real time. Production managers still spend countless hours reviewing dashboards, comparing reports, coordinating maintenance activities, responding to quality deviations, adjusting production schedules, and balancing resource constraints. While analytics platforms have become increasingly sophisticated, many still depend on human operators to interpret information and determine the next action. ### The Top AI Agent Platforms for Industrial Manufacturing #### 1. Plataine: Best AI Agent Platform for Industrial Manufacturing [Plataine](https://www.plataine.com/) approaches industrial AI from the perspective of autonomous manufacturing optimization rather than isolated analytics. Instead of simply helping manufacturers monitor operations, the platform enables AI agents to continuously analyze production conditions, evaluate constraints, and recommend operational decisions across the factory floor. One of Plataine’s strongest differentiators is its ability to connect manufacturing intelligence with real-time operational execution. The platform integrates production schedules, material availability, machine utilization, quality information, and manufacturing workflows to help optimize factory performance continuously. Rather than presenting dashboards that require manual interpretation, Plataine focuses on AI-driven recommendations that improve throughput, reduce waste, and increase operational efficiency. The platform is particularly well suited for complex manufacturing environments where production decisions depend on numerous variables that change throughout the day. Material shortages, machine availability, quality deviations, maintenance activities, and scheduling changes all influence production performance. Plataine’s AI agents continuously evaluate these variables while recommending operational adjustments that help manufacturers maintain productivity. Another advantage is the platform’s focus on autonomous decision support. Manufacturers increasingly want AI that not only identifies problems but also helps coordinate responses across production teams. Plataine supports this transition by enabling AI-assisted planning, production optimization, and factory-wide operational intelligence while keeping engineers and plant managers responsible for final decisions. For organizations pursuing Industry 4.0 initiatives, Plataine offers a comprehensive AI platform capable of improving manufacturing performance across multiple operational domains. #### Core Strengths * AI-driven production optimization * Autonomous manufacturing workflows * Material flow intelligence * Real-time operational decisions * Digital manufacturing optimization * Factory-wide AI coordination #### 2. Sight Machine Sight Machine focuses on manufacturing intelligence by helping organizations unify production data across multiple factories, production lines, and operational systems. Rather than concentrating on individual machines, the platform provides visibility into factory-wide performance, allowing manufacturers to identify process inefficiencies, production bottlenecks, and operational trends that influence overall productivity. highlights.title: BoGuan LLM and 5G-A boosts digital cultural tourism in Xi’an - Developing Telecoms
highlights.titleZh: BoGuan LLM and 5G-A boosts digital cultural tourism in Xi’an - Developing Telecoms
highlights.content: : [Consumer Ecosystems](/telecom-technology/60-consumer-ecosystems.html) ## BoGuan LLM and 5G-A boosts digital cultural tourism in Xi’an Details : Staff Writer : Category: [Consumer Ecosystems](/telecom-technology/60-consumer-ecosystems.html) : 28 views * [Asia-Pacific](/component/tags/tag/asia-pacific.html) * [China](/component/tags/tag/china.html) * [Huawei](/component/tags/tag/huawei.html) * [China Telecom](/component/tags/tag/china-telecom.html) * [5G-Advanced](/component/tags/tag/5g-advanced.html) * [Partner Content](/component/tags/tag/partner-content.html) * [LLMs](/component/tags/tag/llms.html) Less than a year after its commercial launch, the BoGuan multimodal large language model (LLM) designed for cultural tourism in China has entered broad application in Xi’an, and is generating real revenues and value for the tourism industry. As one of the oldest cities in China with a rich cultural heritage, Xi'an is one of the most popular international tourist destinations in China. The city has increasingly turned to digital technologies to enable visitors and scholars to access cultural relics and better engage with the region's rich history whilst also preserving its history. Shaanxi Culture Industry Investment Group (SCG) has been working with partners like Huawei, China Telecom Shaanxi, and China West Airport Group (CWAG) to promote digital and intelligent cultural tourism by leveraging advanced technologies like AI and 5G-A to create numerous new services for visitors. On the AI side of that initiative, SCG and Huawei unveiled the BoGuan LLM in September 2025, billing it as the world's first commercial multimodal LLM for cultural tourism, as well as China's first model dedicated specifically to preserve cultural heritage. **Inside BoGuan** The BoGuan LLM is built on intelligent computing infrastructure and a high-quality dataset that includes over 1.2 PB of data – including 31 million images, 4.4 million minutes of video footage, 2.18 million minutes of audio recordings, 510 3D models, and 960 million pieces of structured text. With that dataset, BoGuan can generate highly-accurate multimodal content, such as museum-quality content about cultural relics. This capability enables it to support creation of new digital relic presentations, digitalization and preservation of traditional craftsmanship, and creation of digital IP for intangible cultural heritage. BoGuan has also been used to develop a range of cultural tourism apps that unlock the business value of quality cultural tourism data. For example, BoGuan powers an AI travel companion agent that had been made available to over 4 million users as of March 2026. Visitors can directly talk with this agent on the GO-SHAANXI app to create and adjust their travel itineraries in Xi’an, and get real-time recommendations for performances at various attractions. Another app created using BoGuan is the Zhiying Camera mini programme – an AI-powered photography service that instantly integrates user photos with AI-generated scenes from history, allowing visitors to "time travel” to ancient times. The BoGuan LLM has also been used to create digital IP such as the popular cartoon character Tang Biaobiao, which was designed by integrating local cultural heritage elements with the stone carvings of the Six Steeds of Zhao Mausoleum. As a testament of Tang Biaobiao’s popularity, sales of related digital collectibles and creative products have exceeded CNY2 million (almost US$295,000). Meanwhile, SCG is also using BoGuan to integrate short drama production with cultural tourism and improve production efficiency and quality in Xi'an, which is also arenowned short drama hub. “Artificial intelligence is not simply a stack of technologies,” said Edric Chu, General Manager of Huawei's Shaanxi Rep Office. “It has become a key enabler that can activate thousands of years of cultural heritage, reshape travel experiences, and inject new momentum into the industry.” **Powered by 5G-A** All of thes...

highlights.content: # AI Agents Redefine Consumer Choices and Brand Value, Survey Finds **[News](HNR-type-type-news.html) - [AI Agents](modules.php?name=HNR&file=topic&tag=AI+Agents)**        [Hotel News Resource](HNR-companyid-coid-10008-Hotel News Resource.html "Hotel News Resource News and Articles") [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=https://www.hotelnewsresource.com/article141948.html "Share on Facebook") [X](https://www.x.com/share?url=https://www.hotelnewsresource.com/article141948.html "Share on X") [Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.hotelnewsresource.com/article141948.html "Share on LinkedIn") [Email](mailto:?subject=Your post title&body=Read complete article in here https://www.hotelnewsresource.com/article141948.html "Share via Email") **In Brief:** A global survey of over 25,000 consumers reveals a significant shift in shopping behavior as people increasingly trust AI agents to make purchasing decisions, challenging traditional brand value and forcing brands to adapt to a new era where machine-driven choices and consumer intent shape market success. * AI Agents Redefine Consumer Choices and Brand Value, Survey Finds - Image Credit Unsplash+ **Introduction: The Rise of AI Agents in Consumer Decision-Making** A recent survey of 25,590 consumers across 16 countries indicates a major change in how people interact with brands and make shopping decisions. With the proliferation of options and increasing demands on their time, consumers are turning to AI agents to handle routine tasks, make purchase decisions, and even shop autonomously. This trend is expected to reshape the landscape of brand value, requiring brands and retailers to adapt to new expectations and methods of consumer engagement. **Survey Findings: Trust in AI Agents Surpasses Human Advice** The study found that 74% of respondents would trust a personal AI agent more than their best friend to make a purchase on their behalf. This trust extends beyond simple recommendations; consumers are delegating a range of shopping activities to AI, from executing specific tasks to making autonomous purchases. While 74% would let an AI agent handle routine tasks, 32% are comfortable allowing agents to make purchase decisions within set parameters, and 9% are ready for fully autonomous shopping. Despite this willingness to delegate, consumers retain control over purchases that carry personal meaning. More than half (56%) would instruct their agent on which brands to consider, but 37% of loyal consumers would allow agents to switch brands if a better fit is found. This suggests that while loyalty persists, it is increasingly conditional and mediated by AI. **Key Definitions: Understanding the New Shopping Ecosystem** The report distinguishes between generative AI assistants, which respond to user prompts, and AI agents, which act on behalf of consumers within defined boundaries. The concept of a "delegation dial" describes the varying degrees to which consumers hand over tasks or decisions to AI, ranging from executing instructions to making independent purchases. The "agentic ecosystem" encompasses the network of platforms, retailers, brands, and data providers that support these agents. **Stages of Delegation: From Task Execution to Autonomous Purchasing** Consumers are adjusting how much control they give to AI agents based on the type of purchase and its personal significance. For routine tasks like negotiating deals or managing returns, 74% are comfortable delegating to an agent. For more significant decisions, such as planning a family holiday, 32% are open to agent involvement but prefer to approve the final choice. Only 9% are willing to let agents make autonomous purchases, though this figure could rise as consumers gain confidence through low-risk transactions. The type of purchase influences delegation. Recurring services and routine replenishments lead to greater openness to AI involvement, while lifestyle and travel purchases ...

highlights.content: Thursday, July 2, 2026 Copyright 2021 - All Right Reserved [AI Infrastructure](https://www.rcrwireless.com/category/ai-infrastructure)[IoT](https://www.rcrwireless.com/category/internet-of-things-4)[Network Infrastructure](https://www.rcrwireless.com/category/network-infrastructure)[Opinion](https://www.rcrwireless.com/category/opinion)[Private 5G](https://www.rcrwireless.com/category/private-5g)[Private Networks](https://www.rcrwireless.com/category/private-networks)[Telco AI](https://www.rcrwireless.com/category/ai) # Wednesday | Telco agents and smash hits (editorial commentary) by [James Blackman](https://www.rcrwireless.com/author/jblackman) written by [James Blackman](https://www.rcrwireless.com/author/jblackman) [Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.rcrwireless.com%2F20260702%2Fnetwork-infrastructure%2Ftelco-agents-smash-hits&title=Wednesday%20%7C%20Telco%20agents%20and%20smash%20hits%20%28editorial%20commentary%29)[Email](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#) 0[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.rcrwireless.com%2F20260702%2Fnetwork-infrastructure%2Ftelco-agents-smash-hits&title=Wednesday%20%7C%20Telco%20agents%20and%20smash%20hits%20%28editorial%20commentary%29)[Email](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#) *2* *From the [newsletter](https://rcrtech.com/rcrwirelessnews/control-platforms-and-smash-hits/): AI agents in telecoms is sharpening focus on governance, controlled autonomy and human oversight, while momentum in private 5G and industrial AI continues to build through new deployments, ecosystem partnerships, spectrum initiatives and innovative network applications.* Firstly, good stuff from Sean Kinney, as always – from Cisco Live a couple of weeks back (with apologies for its tardiness) and DTW last week (right on time). The two pieces are worth taking as a pair: they discuss the tension as AI reshapes operational control in networks. The DTW writeup, an intellectual take, says [probabilistic AI and deterministic telecoms are strange bedfellows](https://rcrtech.com/analyst-angle/core-tension-telco-ai/) – that inference and judgement are unreliable stewards of repeatability and predictability. Which is the discussion most industries are having about AI, actually. The Cisco review deals with the same, effectively: as AI is licensed to take action, rather than just to generate responses, [mechanisms must be in place to govern what agents can do](https://rcrtech.com/analyst-angle/cisco-agentic-ai/), what they can access, and how their actions are monitored. Which is the Cisco pitch. The point is, the telco AI story – just the industrialisation of AI, broadly – is about human control of machine intelligence, and controlled autonomy rather than unrestricted autonomy. Search-out the two articles. Secondly, and related (to AI-organized control of industrial systems, plus outward-looking telco upsides), there is lots happening in the private 5G space. Twelve months ago, *RCR* might have covered these items individually, in depth, but AI has changed everything, including the news trade and news beat (*so share if you care).* For one, Nokia – which we shall call ECE for now, on the grounds Nokia has put it up for sale, and doesn’t deserve t...

highlights.title: Z.ai launches ZCode to challenge Cursor, Claude Code and GitHub Copilot in AI coding - Venturebeat
highlights.titleZh: Z.ai launches ZCode to challenge Cursor, Claude Code and GitHub Copilot in AI coding - Venturebeat
highlights.content: # Z.ai launches ZCode to challenge Cursor, Claude Code and GitHub Copilot in AI coding [Michael Nuñez](/author/michael_nunez) [Z.ai](http://z.ai), the Beijing-based artificial intelligence lab formerly known as Zhipu AI, on Wednesday officially launched [ZCode](https://zcode.z.ai/), a free desktop application it describes as an "Agentic Development Environment" purpose-built for its flagship [GLM-5.2](https://z.ai/blog/glm-5.2) large language model. The move marks the company's most aggressive push yet into the fast-growing AI-powered coding tool market, where it now competes directly with [Cursor](https://cursor.com/get-started), [Claude Code](https://www.anthropic.com/product/claude-code), [GitHub Copilot](https://github.com/features/copilot), and [Google's Antigravity](https://antigravity.google/). "Introducing ZCode, the official development environment for GLM-5.2," the company wrote on X, noting the tool is available on macOS, Windows, and Linux, supports bring-your-own-key (BYOK) configurations for third-party models, and offers a 1.5x usage-quota bonus for subscribers to its GLM Coding Plan. Read one way, [ZCode](https://zcode.z.ai/) is simply another entrant in a crowded market. Read another, it is a single product that crystallizes three of the most consequential trends in enterprise software today: the race-to-the-bottom pricing of frontier AI models, the geopolitical balkanization of the AI stack, and the rapid maturation of agentic coding agents into what Gartner now estimates is a [roughly $10 billion market](https://enterprisedna.co/resources/news/gartner-enterprise-ai-coding-agents-10-billion-market-2026/). ## **An AI coding tool designed to think in projects, not prompts** Unlike traditional IDEs that bolt on AI through a chat sidebar or autocomplete extension, [ZCode](https://zcode.z.ai/) is best understood as an agent-first development environment. Its core design is built around long-horizon tasks: the user describes an outcome, the agent plans the work, edits files, runs checks, reviews progress, and continues across multiple iterations until the goal is met. [ZCode](https://zcode.z.ai/) organizes the development experience around the [ZCode Agent](https://zcode.z.ai/en), deeply tuned for [GLM-5.2](https://z.ai/blog/glm-5.2), with emphasis on deep integration: the model, tools, and execution workflow are tuned together so the Agent fits continuous, multi-step real-world development tasks. The environment supports continuous follow-up across devices: desktop, mobile Remote, and Feishu / WeChat Bot can all keep the same workspace task moving. Sensitive commands, file changes, and high-permission actions go through confirmation before execution. That remote-control feature — the ability to steer a running coding agent from [WeChat](https://www.wechat.com/en), [Feishu](https://baike.baidu.com/en/item/Feishu/14594), or [Telegram](https://web.telegram.org/) on a phone — is a differentiator that speaks directly to the Chinese developer market, where those messaging platforms dominate professional communication. You can keep checking progress and adding instructions while long-running work continues, from any device with these messaging apps. The tool is free to download. Revenue flows through Z.ai's [GLM Coding Plan subscription tiers](https://z.ai/subscribe), which start at $16.20 per month for a "Lite" plan and scale to $144 per month for "Max" — prices that undercut Anthropic's Claude Code and Cursor's comparable tiers by significant margins. Through July 31, [ZCode](https://zcode.z.ai/) is offering a promotional 1.5x effective quota bonus for Coding Plan subscribers, with off-peak token consumption charged at a 0.67x coefficient. The platform also supports multiple AI models and agents, including Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, and OpenCode — a pragmatic concession to the reality that no single model wins every task. ## **GLM-5.2, the open-source model trained entirely on Chinese chips, powers the whole experienc...

highlights.title: New partnership supports sovereign AI in Saudi Arabia - Developing Telecoms
highlights.titleZh: New partnership supports sovereign AI in Saudi Arabia - Developing Telecoms
highlights.content: : [Data Centres & Networks](/telecom-technology/data-centres-networks.html) ## New partnership supports sovereign AI in Saudi Arabia Details : Vaughan O'Grady : Category: [Data Centres & Networks](/telecom-technology/data-centres-networks.html) : 11 views * [Middle East North Africa](/component/tags/tag/middle-east-north-africa.html) * [Data Centres](/component/tags/tag/data-centres.html) * [Saudi Arabia](/component/tags/tag/saudi-arabia.html) * [Digital Transformation](/component/tags/tag/digital-transformation.html) * [Wistron](/component/tags/tag/wistron.html) * [NVIDIA](/component/tags/tag/nvidia.html) * [Sovereign AI](/component/tags/tag/sovereign-ai.html) * [Magna AI](/component/tags/tag/magna-ai.html) * [Trend Micro](/component/tags/tag/trend-micro.html) * [Emaar Executive Company](/component/tags/tag/emaar-executive-company.html) A new partnership has been announced that will build sovereign AI data centre infrastructure in Saudi Arabia. To undertake this work, Magna AI, a global integrated-value-chain sovereign AI transformation company established through a partnership between Trend Micro and Wistron Digital Technology Holding Company (WDH) and powered by Nvidia, has announced a strategic collaboration with Emaar Executive Company (EEC), a Saudi-based company specialising in data centre construction, engineering, procurement and construction (EPC), systems integration, and infrastructure services. The partners explain that, as Saudi Arabia continues to advance its national digital transformation agenda, the need for trusted AI infrastructure is becoming increasingly central to the Kingdom's long-term competitiveness. For both nations and organisations, AI sovereignty means keeping data, models and the value they generate under local control rather than dependent on platforms owned elsewhere. They add that sovereign AI data centres and AI factories are expected to play a critical role in enabling organisations to train, fine-tune, deploy and operate advanced AI models while maintaining control over data, workloads, governance and compliance. As part of the agreement, Magna AI will lead AI factory architecture, platform development, and AI security and governance, while Emaar Executive Company will deliver the engineering and construction backbone, including EPC execution, data centre construction, civil, mechanical, electrical and low-voltage systems integration, on-site project management, facility support, and regulatory coordination. EEC says its capabilities are supported by ISO 9001, ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 certifications and a dedicated in-Kingdom workforce. Together, the companies aim to support the deployment of secure, scalable, and locally aligned AI infrastructure for government entities, regulated industries, enterprises, startups and academic institutions. The collaboration is expected to span from the earliest stages of infrastructure development through to long-term operation. This includes site identification, feasibility studies and business-case development; systems integration for AI data centres; energy, cooling and sustainability optimisation; and secure, compliant operations aligned with Saudi data-residency and cybersecurity requirements. ## More Articles you may be Interested in... #### [Another India-Southeast Asia undersea cable on the way](/telecom-technology/optical-fixed-networks/20472-another-india-southeast-asia-undersea-cable-on-the-way.html) Jul 02, 2026 Vaughan O'Grady #### [Elea and AXIA Energia plan neutral AI data centre in Amazon region](/telecom-technology/data-centres-networks/20468-elea-and-axia-energia-plan-neutral-ai-data-centre-in-amazon-region.html) Jul 02, 2026 John Tanner #### [Zain to take over former MTN network in Syria](/telecom-business/telecom-investment-mergers/20465-zain-to-take-over-former-mtn-network-in-syria.html) Jul 01, 2026 Vaughan O'Grady #### [Thales Alenia to supply software-defined satellite to Es’hailSat](/telecom-technology/satellite-communications-net...

highlights.content: [Skip to content](#main-content) [LDS Let's Data ScienceLEARN • BUILD • STAY AHEAD](/) [Sign in](/login?redirect=%2Fnews%2Fbase44-launches-base1-to-improve-ui-generation-3728d950) Products & Toolsbase44base1vibe codingwix # Base44 launches Base1 to improve UI generation ||By [LDS Team](/editorial-standards) 6.9 Relevance Score Specialized models trained on product interaction data can change UX quality, latency, and cost trade-offs compared with generalist frontier models. According to Wix's corporate blog, Base44, acquired by Wix in June 2025, has launched a proprietary LLM called Base1 to power its vibe-coding app-creation workflow. TechCrunch and other outlets report the model was trained on a dataset derived from "tens of millions of real user interactions on the platform" (TechCrunch) and that Base44 will use reinforcement learning in ongoing tuning (Business Insider). Business Insider also quotes founder Maor Shlomo describing the motivation as avoiding the "generic" UI outputs common from frontier models. Reporting places the move in a broader pattern of startups building in-house models to reduce inference costs and increase product defensibility (TechCrunch, Aixploria). ### Editorial analysis Practitioners building UX- or design-sensitive applications should treat this as an example of the trade-offs between using frontier, generalist models and investing in a narrow, product-specific LLM. Specialized models can materially improve surface-level outputs (layout, visual style) while offering levers on latency and inference cost that are hard to obtain when routing every request to a third-party general model. **What happened**, Reported facts: According to Wix's corporate blog, Base44 launched a proprietary LLM called **`Base1`** to support its vibe-coding app-creation experience (Wix blog). TechCrunch reports the model was developed from a dataset generated by **"tens of millions of real user interactions on the platform"** and that the release is being rolled out to users (TechCrunch). Business Insider quotes Base44 founder Maor Shlomo saying part of the rationale was to combat generic UI outputs: "everybody feels like they're getting the same UI when they're coding with the general models" (Business Insider). Multiple outlets note that Base44 was acquired by Wix for **$80 million** in June 2025 (Business Insider, Aixploria). **Technical context and training approach**, Reported facts and sourcing: TechCrunch and Aixploria describe Base1 as a model fine-tuned and trained on Base44's product data, with reinforcement learning used to push the model toward more varied, distinctive UI generations (TechCrunch; Business Insider). Reporting indicates Base44 previously used external models such as OpenAI and Anthropic, and has added Base1 to a model selector that includes GPT-5.5 and Claude's Opus 4.8 (Business Insider). ### Industry context Public reporting frames this move within a wider pattern where AI products with sufficient user interactions and revenue consider owning model stacks to control costs and output quality (TechCrunch). Investors and analysts quoted in coverage, including Headline partner Jonathan Userovici, place data, distribution, and tech stack as the three ingredients of defensibility for AI startups (TechCrunch). **What this means for builders**, Editorial analysis: Teams that rely on generative models for UI or other high-dimension creative outputs should evaluate whether a narrow model trained on in-product signals will deliver better end-user variability and reduce repetitive "AI slop" outputs. The trade-off is nontrivial: owning a model adds operational complexity (training, RL loops, inference deployment), but reporting from Wix emphasizes lower inference cost and faster iteration cadence as key benefits (Wix blog). **Competitive and market signals**, Reported facts and synthesis: Coverage names competitors in the vibe-coding and app-creation space such as Lovable, Replit, and Cursor, and frames B...

highlights.titleZh: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung - TechCrunch
highlights.content: ### Topics [Biotech & Health](/category/biotech-health/) [Cloud Computing](/tag/cloud-computing/) [Commerce](/category/commerce/) [Crypto](/category/cryptocurrency/) [Enterprise](/category/enterprise/) [EVs](/tag/evs/) [Fintech](/category/fintech/) [Fundraising](/category/fundraising/) [Gadgets](/category/gadgets/) [Gaming](/category/gaming/) [Google](/tag/google/) [Government & Policy](/category/government-policy/) [Hardware](/category/hardware/) [Instagram](/tag/instagram/) [Layoffs](/tag/layoffs/) [Media & Entertainment](/category/media-entertainment/) [Meta](/tag/meta/) [Microsoft](/tag/microsoft/) [Privacy](/category/privacy/) [Robotics](/category/robotics/) [Security](/category/security/) [Social](/category/social/) [Space](/category/space/) [Startups](/category/startups/) [TikTok](/tag/tiktok/) [Transportation](/category/transportation/) [Venture](/category/venture/) ### More from TechCrunch [Staff](/about-techcrunch/) [Events](/events/) [Startup Battlefield](/startup-battlefield/) [StrictlyVC](https://strictlyvc.com/) [Newsletters](/newsletters/) [Podcasts](/podcasts/) [Videos](/video/) [Partner Content](/sponsored/) [TechCrunch Brand Studio](/brand-studio/) [Crunchboard](https://www.crunchboard.com/) [Contact Us](/contact-us/) [AI](https://techcrunch.com/category/artificial-intelligence/) # Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung [Lucas Ropek](https://techcrunch.com/author/lucas-ropek/) Back in April, Reuters [reported that](https://www.reuters.com/business/anthropic-weighs-building-it-own-ai-chips-sources-say-2026-04-09/) Anthropic was toying with the idea of producing its own AI chips as a means of responding to chip shortages. Now, it would appear that the company is getting serious about this idea. On Thursday, [The Information reported](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/anthropic-talks-samsung-manufacture-custom-ai-chip) that Anthropic was in contact with Samsung to explore a collaboration around the pending chip. However, Anthropic hasn’t yet decided what the chip will be used for, how it will fit into the server, or how powerful it will be, according to the report. When reached for comment, Anthropic told TechCrunch that a diversified hardware stack that includes chips from Google, Amazon, and Nvidia will continue to be pivotal to its compute strategy. On the topic of a potential Samsung partnership, the company said it had nothing further to add. A number of AI companies have sought to develop custom chips — both as a way to create unique hardware for specific compute tasks and to gain a certain amount of independence from Nvidia, which continues to be the undisputed leader of the chip industry. Anthropic’s announcement may also be a response to one made last week by its key competitor, OpenAI, which has [teamed up with Broadcom](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/24/openai-unveils-its-first-custom-chip-built-by-broadcom/) to announce its own custom built inference processor, dubbed “Jalapeño.” OpenAI says that the chip is more efficient, demonstrating better performance-per-watt, than other competitor chips. [Amazon](https://aws.amazon.com/ai/machine-learning/trainium/) and [Google](https://cloud.google.com/tpu) both offer custom-built TPUs as part of their cloud offering. Samsung is already embedded in the AI industry, and acts as a major partner of Nvidia, [producing chips](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/samsung-elec-showcases-nvidias-inference-222001364.html) that the company needs to train or run its AI models. In turn, Samsung uses Nvidia’s software to manufacture its chips. The duo are [working on an AI chip factory](https://investor.nvidia.com/news/press-release-details/2025/NVIDIA-and-Samsung-Build-AI-Factory-to-Transform-Global-Intelligent-Manufacturing/default.aspx) in South Korea. Samsung has also [discussed partnering](https://www.koreajoongangdaily.com/business/samsung-poised-to-make-part-of-googles-next-ai-chip-as-tsmc-nears-capacity/12713503) with Google on its chip-making effo...

AI Agents.title: New AI Agents Pose ‘Existential Threat’ to How Grants Are Awarded - Inside Higher Ed
AI Agents.titleZh: New AI Agents Pose ‘Existential Threat’ to How Grants Are Awarded - Inside Higher Ed
AI Agents.content: [News](/news) * [Faculty](/news/faculty) * [Research](/news/faculty/research) July 02, 2026 # New AI Agents Pose ‘Existential Threat’ to How Grants Are Awarded The rapid development of technology is outpacing any attempts to reform assessment systems, researchers warn. By  [Seher Asaf for Times Higher Education](/author/seher-asaf-times-higher-education) The rise of more sophisticated artificial intelligence agents poses an “existential threat” to the way research funding is awarded, experts have warned, saying autonomous AI systems could further flood grant competitions with applications, making it harder to identify the best ideas. Speaking during a webinar organized by the League of European Research Universities, Geraint Rees, vice provost for research, innovation and global engagement at UCL, said a new wave of AI tools represents a fundamental shift from today’s widely used generative AI. Unlike large language models such as ChatGPT and Claude, AI agents can autonomously gather information, make decisions and produce work with minimal human oversight. #### Most Popular * [ED Issues New List of Professional Degrees After Court Order](https://www.insidehighered.com/news/quick-takes/2026/06/30/ed-issues-new-list-professional-degrees-after-court-order) * [U Tennessee to Pay Millions to Prof Fired for Kirk Comments](https://www.insidehighered.com/news/quick-takes/2026/07/01/u-tennessee-pay-millions-prof-fired-kirk-comments) * [Free Tuition for Adult Community College Learners](https://www.insidehighered.com/news/student-success/academic-life/2026/06/29/free-tuition-adult-community-college-learners) “In the context of grants, generative AI may help you polish or write a better application,” Rees said. “But agentic AI will go off and write the application and submit it for you.” Such systems can be trained on a researcher’s published work, funding criteria and previously successful grant applications to generate, review and improve proposals. “The marginal cost of producing an application falls to zero,” Rees said. “That’s important because it changes the nature of the problem. It’s not about polishing your grant. It’s about a system that’s designed fundamentally to assess human judgment and human ideas being fed outputs that simulate human judgment.” [Advertisement](https://solutions.insidehighered.com/branding) Rees and James Wilsdon, a professor of research policy at UCL and executive director of the Research on Research Institute, said AI agents are creating three major challenges for research funders—a massive increase in applications, “quality compression” and “convergence.” The pair’s [recent research](https://www.timeshighereducation.com/news/research-funders-flooded-ai-assisted-applications) analyzed data from 12 funders across seven research systems and found grant application volumes had increased by 57 percent between the launch of ChatGPT in 2022 and the end of 2025. Early data for 2026 suggests the rate of growth is accelerating even more, Wilsdon said. The second challenge is what the researchers described as “quality compression.” [Advertisement](https://solutions.insidehighered.com/branding) “When everyone’s proposal has optimization and great writing, the quality floor rises, but the ceiling stays the same,” Rees said. “It becomes harder to discriminate between really excellent ideas and ones that are just OK.” The longer-term concern is convergence, where AI systems generate grant applications while also assisting reviewers in assessing them. “If you’ve got agents writing, and agents reviewing, and the agents are talking to each other, they’re all trained on exactly the same work,” Rees said. “Over time, the system isn’t going to evaluate great ideas. It’s just going to measure how well agents simulate what funders have previously rewarded. That’s a real existential threat to what we’re trying to do.” #### Editor's Picks * [The Researcher Who Spent Years Studying Grass for the World Cup](/news/faculty/researc...

Over the past decade, factories invested heavily in IoT sensors, Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES), industrial analytics, and predictive maintenance solutions.** These technologies generated unprecedented operational visibility, allowing manufacturers to monitor equipment, production lines, quality metrics, and material flows in real time. Production managers still spend countless hours reviewing dashboards, comparing reports, coordinating maintenance activities, responding to quality deviations, adjusting production schedules, and balancing resource constraints. While analytics platforms have become increasingly sophisticated, many still depend on human operators to interpret information and determine the next action. ### The Top AI Agent Platforms for Industrial Manufacturing #### 1. Plataine: Best AI Agent Platform for Industrial Manufacturing [Plataine](https://www.plataine.com/) approaches industrial AI from the perspective of autonomous manufacturing optimization rather than isolated analytics. Instead of simply helping manufacturers monitor operations, the platform enables AI agents to continuously analyze production conditions, evaluate constraints, and recommend operational decisions across the factory floor. One of Plataine’s strongest differentiators is its ability to connect manufacturing intelligence with real-time operational execution. The platform integrates production schedules, material availability, machine utilization, quality information, and manufacturing workflows to help optimize factory performance continuously. Rather than presenting dashboards that require manual interpretation, Plataine focuses on AI-driven recommendations that improve throughput, reduce waste, and increase operational efficiency. The platform is particularly well suited for complex manufacturing environments where production decisions depend on numerous variables that change throughout the day. Material shortages, machine availability, quality deviations, maintenance activities, and scheduling changes all influence production performance. Plataine’s AI agents continuously evaluate these variables while recommending operational adjustments that help manufacturers maintain productivity. Another advantage is the platform’s focus on autonomous decision support. Manufacturers increasingly want AI that not only identifies problems but also helps coordinate responses across production teams. Plataine supports this transition by enabling AI-assisted planning, production optimization, and factory-wide operational intelligence while keeping engineers and plant managers responsible for final decisions. For organizations pursuing Industry 4.0 initiatives, Plataine offers a comprehensive AI platform capable of improving manufacturing performance across multiple operational domains. #### Core Strengths * AI-driven production optimization * Autonomous manufacturing workflows * Material flow intelligence * Real-time operational decisions * Digital manufacturing optimization * Factory-wide AI coordination #### 2. Sight Machine Sight Machine focuses on manufacturing intelligence by helping organizations unify production data across multiple factories, production lines, and operational systems. Rather than concentrating on individual machines, the platform provides visibility into factory-wide performance, allowing manufacturers to identify process inefficiencies, production bottlenecks, and operational trends that influence overall productivity. AI Agents.content: # AI Agents Redefine Consumer Choices and Brand Value, Survey Finds **[News](HNR-type-type-news.html) - [AI Agents](modules.php?name=HNR&file=topic&tag=AI+Agents)**        [Hotel News Resource](HNR-companyid-coid-10008-Hotel News Resource.html "Hotel News Resource News and Articles") [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=https://www.hotelnewsresource.com/article141948.html "Share on Facebook") [X](https://www.x.com/share?url=https://www.hotelnewsresource.com/article141948.html "Share on X") [Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.hotelnewsresource.com/article141948.html "Share on LinkedIn") [Email](mailto:?subject=Your post title&body=Read complete article in here https://www.hotelnewsresource.com/article141948.html "Share via Email") **In Brief:** A global survey of over 25,000 consumers reveals a significant shift in shopping behavior as people increasingly trust AI agents to make purchasing decisions, challenging traditional brand value and forcing brands to adapt to a new era where machine-driven choices and consumer intent shape market success. * AI Agents Redefine Consumer Choices and Brand Value, Survey Finds - Image Credit Unsplash+ **Introduction: The Rise of AI Agents in Consumer Decision-Making** A recent survey of 25,590 consumers across 16 countries indicates a major change in how people interact with brands and make shopping decisions. With the proliferation of options and increasing demands on their time, consumers are turning to AI agents to handle routine tasks, make purchase decisions, and even shop autonomously. This trend is expected to reshape the landscape of brand value, requiring brands and retailers to adapt to new expectations and methods of consumer engagement. **Survey Findings: Trust in AI Agents Surpasses Human Advice** The study found that 74% of respondents would trust a personal AI agent more than their best friend to make a purchase on their behalf. This trust extends beyond simple recommendations; consumers are delegating a range of shopping activities to AI, from executing specific tasks to making autonomous purchases. While 74% would let an AI agent handle routine tasks, 32% are comfortable allowing agents to make purchase decisions within set parameters, and 9% are ready for fully autonomous shopping. Despite this willingness to delegate, consumers retain control over purchases that carry personal meaning. More than half (56%) would instruct their agent on which brands to consider, but 37% of loyal consumers would allow agents to switch brands if a better fit is found. This suggests that while loyalty persists, it is increasingly conditional and mediated by AI. **Key Definitions: Understanding the New Shopping Ecosystem** The report distinguishes between generative AI assistants, which respond to user prompts, and AI agents, which act on behalf of consumers within defined boundaries. The concept of a "delegation dial" describes the varying degrees to which consumers hand over tasks or decisions to AI, ranging from executing instructions to making independent purchases. The "agentic ecosystem" encompasses the network of platforms, retailers, brands, and data providers that support these agents. **Stages of Delegation: From Task Execution to Autonomous Purchasing** Consumers are adjusting how much control they give to AI agents based on the type of purchase and its personal significance. For routine tasks like negotiating deals or managing returns, 74% are comfortable delegating to an agent. For more significant decisions, such as planning a family holiday, 32% are open to agent involvement but prefer to approve the final choice. Only 9% are willing to let agents make autonomous purchases, though this figure could rise as consumers gain confidence through low-risk transactions. The type of purchase influences delegation. Recurring services and routine replenishments lead to greater openness to AI involvement, while lifestyle and travel purchases ...

The two pieces are worth taking as a pair: they discuss the tension as AI reshapes operational control in networks. The DTW writeup, an intellectual take, says [probabilistic AI and deterministic telecoms are strange bedfellows](https://rcrtech.com/analyst-angle/core-tension-telco-ai/) – that inference and judgement are unreliable stewards of repeatability and predictability. Which is the discussion most industries are having about AI, actually. The Cisco review deals with the same, effectively: as AI is licensed to take action, rather than just to generate responses, [mechanisms must be in place to govern what agents can do](https://rcrtech.com/analyst-angle/cisco-agentic-ai/), what they can access, and how their actions are monitored. Which is the Cisco pitch. The point is, the telco AI story – just the industrialisation of AI, broadly – is about human control of machine intelligence, and controlled autonomy rather than unrestricted autonomy. Search-out the two articles. Secondly, and related (to AI-organized control of industrial systems, plus outward-looking telco upsides), there is lots happening in the private 5G space. Twelve months ago, *RCR* might have covered these items individually, in depth, but AI has changed everything, including the news trade and news beat (*so share if you care).* For one, Nokia – which we shall call ECE for now, on the grounds Nokia has put it up for sale, and doesn’t deserve t...

AI Agents.title: UK start-up Macro Technologies launches AI decision engine for asset managers - FinTech Futures
AI Agents.titleZh: UK start-up Macro Technologies launches AI decision engine for asset managers - FinTech Futures
AI Agents.content: * [Fintech Start-ups](/fintech/fintech-start-ups) * [AI in Fintech](/fintech/ai-in-fintech) * [WealthTech](/wealthtech) * [Investment Management](/wealthtech/investment-management) * [News](/latest-news) # UK start-up Macro Technologies launches AI decision engine for asset managers Macro, led by co-founder Jaime Villa, has debuted The Macro Analyst, its first agentic AI product for asset management. [Cameron Emanuel-Burns,](/author/cameron-emanuel-burns) Reporter, FinTech Futures July 2, 2026 schedule 2 Min Read Macro Technologies, a London-based start-up building agentic AI infrastructure for asset management, has officially launched with its first product, The Macro Analyst. In discussion with FinTech Futures , principal co-founder Jaime Villa explains that the new offering functions as a "decision engine for portfolio managers" able to ingest central-bank communication, macro data, market pricing, and research. It then scores that evidence, updates the implied policy path, compares it with what the market has priced, and keeps a "cited, replayable record behind every conclusion". Villa, formerly of [Citadel Securities](/venture-capital-funding/kraken-raises-800m-files-for-us-ipo) , says that the product is capable of automating the "repeatable analytical work that macro research analysts and strategists do today, so the portfolio manager's judgment is freed for the decision itself and the firm keeps institutional memory when people move on". The founder claims that what sets the company apart from its competitors is its "three-corpus evidence chain: our scored macro-state corpus, attributed external research and market context, and each client's private memory, fused into one governed, cited workflow". Related: [June 2026: Top five new launch stories of the month](/fintech-start-ups/june-2026-top-five-new-launch-stories-of-the-month) Additionally, Villa also contends that Macro's use of frontier models provides differentiation by equipping the model with macro-specific evidence that it would not otherwise possess. He argues that while general models such as Claude or ChatGPT can summarise regulatory communications, "Macro Analyst tells you whether the policy distribution moved, who moved it, where the curve disagrees, and whether your thesis still holds." Macro currently operates as a founder-led team of two. Villa's partner remains in stealth mode but brings a decade of experience across Rokos Capital Management, Trafigura, and [Citi](/job-cuts-new-hires/tech-veteran-brian-saluzzo-named-new-citi-cio) . The start-up is currently independently funded by its co-founders; however, as the company looks to scale, Villa describes a "two-track approach". "On the one hand, discussing strategic partnerships with Tier 1 hedge funds globally, as firms are all looking to accelerate their AI deployment. There are also conversations with independent and venture capital investors."   Moving forward, Villa outlines a broader vision of one platform with several vertical agents. "The Macro Analyst proves the pattern in macro, and the same engine extends into equities, already in development, and later commodities, toward one consolidated cross-asset workbench." Read more about: [UK](/keyword/uk) [Macro Technologies](/keyword/macro-technologies) ## About the Author [Cameron Emanuel-Burns](/author/cameron-emanuel-burns) Reporter, FinTech Futures Cameron Emanuel-Burns, a reporter at FinTech Futures, is dedicated to enhancing the accessibility of fintech for a broader audience. He has contributed articles to various media platforms and possesses a strong interest in all aspects of fintech. Prior to his involvement in the financial industry, Emanuel-Burns worked in the podcasting field, where he held editorial, hosting,and production positions for renowned production companies, covering diverse topics. With a research master's degree in conflict analysis, he conducted a study on the influence of anti-refugee policies on extremism. [See more from...

AI Agents.title: Meta is finally catching up to OpenAI, its AI leader says - Business Insider
AI Agents.titleZh: Meta is finally catching up to OpenAI, its AI leader says - Business Insider
AI Agents.content: [Subscribe](https://www.businessinsider.com/subscription) [Newsletters](https://www.businessinsider.com/subscription/newsletter) Exclusive # Alexandr Wang says Meta's coming AI has caught up with OpenAI's flagship model By [Charles Rollet](https://www.businessinsider.com/author/charles-rollet) You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email. and [Pranav Dixit](https://www.businessinsider.com/author/pranav-dixit) You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email. [Become an Insider](/subscription) and start reading now. Have an account? . Meta is making significant progress in the AI model race, its superintelligence chief Alexandr Wang told employees today. In an [internal town hall,](https://www.businessinsider.com/zuckerberg-said-metas-ai-progress-has-been-slower-than-expected-2026-7) Wang said that Meta's upcoming AI model — which is codenamed Watermelon — has caught up with OpenAI's flagship GPT-5.5 model, he said, according to two sources familiar with the matter. Wang cited the achievement based on closely followed AI model benchmarks. It's not clear which benchmarks Wang cited. "Watermelon, our next model after Avocado, is currently in training," Wang said in the town hall, according to a person familiar with the matter. "Watermelon uses an order of magnitude more compute than Avocado," he added, referring to Meta's internal codename for Muse Spark, the first in a family of models that the company [released](https://www.businessinsider.com/muse-spark-meta-ai-model-2026-4) in April. Meta's AI ambitions have long hinged on a simple goal: closing the gap with OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic. Despite a massive investment in chips, data centers, and talent, the company has struggled to convince developers and customers that its models belong at the industry's leading edge. If Wang's assessment is accurate, it would mark the clearest sign yet that Meta's investment and Zuckerberg's aggressive talent blitz are beginning to pay off, even as the race continues to move at a rapid pace. GPT 5.5 is a powerful chatbot that [OpenAI released](https://www.businessinsider.com/inside-openai-exclusive-launch-party-chatgpt-planned-itself-2026-5) in April of this year. OpenAI then debuted its most powerful model yet, GPT 5.6, late last month, but [hasn't released it generally yet,](https://www.businessinsider.com/openai-gpt-5-6-limited-preview-us-government-ai-security-2026-6) based on the US government's requests. Meta declined to comment. OpenAI didn't respond to a request for comment. [### Explore BI Games Take a smarter break in your day - and see how far you get. Play now](/games) In April, Meta [released the first in a series of models called Muse Spark,](https://www.businessinsider.com/muse-spark-meta-ai-model-2026-4) which performed well on benchmarks but did not match or exceed OpenAI or other labs such as Anthropic. Zuckerberg is ferociously pushing for Meta to get ahead in the AI race. He appointed Wang last year to head this effort, [renaming the company's AI division](https://www.businessi...

LLMs.title: A new, inexpensive Chinese AI model is catching up with Anthropic, OpenAI on their home turf - Reuters
LLMs.titleZh: A new, inexpensive Chinese AI model is catching up with Anthropic, OpenAI on their home turf - Reuters
LLMs.content: [Skip to main content](#main-content) # A new, inexpensive Chinese AI model is catching up with Anthropic, OpenAI on their home turf By [Laurie Chen](/authors/laurie-chen/) and [Aditya Soni](/authors/aditya-soni/) Item 1 of 2 OpenAI CEO Sam Altman attends an event in Tokyo, Japan February 3, 2025. REUTERS/Kim Kyung-Hoon/File Photo **[1/2]**OpenAI CEO Sam Altman attends an event in Tokyo, Japan February 3, 2025. REUTERS/Kim Kyung-Hoon/File Photo [Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab](https://www.reutersconnect.com/item/anthropic-v-openai-behind-the-bitter-battle-for-the-future-of-ai/dGFnOnJldXRlcnMuY29tLDIwMjU6bmV3c21sX1JDMlVNQ0E0QzNaMQ%3D%3D/?utm_medium=rcom-article-media&utm_campaign=rcom-rcp-lead) * Summary * Companies * There is growing interest from Western startups in switching to cheaper Chinese AI * Z.ai's GLM-5.2 performs nearly as well as Claude Opus 4.8 * Western enterprise adoption complicated by data security concerns BEIJING/BENGALURU, July 2 (Reuters) - Since DeepSeek shocked markets early last year with its cheap but powerful AI model, global consumers have been faced with a choice: Chinese offerings with lower prices and less capability or OpenAI or Anthropic, which have poured ​billions into development. A model called [GLM-5.2](https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/after-anthropic-shutdown-chinas-zai-closes-frontier-gap-it-plans-dual-listing-2026-06-25/), launched last month by Beijing-based startup Z.ai, may finally be closing that gap in terms of Western interest. Make sense of global markets with the Trading Day newsletter. Sign up [here.](/newsletters/trading-day/?location=article-paragraph&redirectUrl=%2Fworld%2Fchina%2Fa-new-inexpensive-chinese-ai-model-is-catching-up-with-anthropic-openai-their-2026-07-02%2F) GLM-5.2 has Silicon Valley buzzing with its coding and ‌agent capabilities, or the ability to execute complex tasks with minimal prompting, that almost rival leading U.S. offerings at a fraction of the cost, in what some experts are calling a "mini DeepSeek moment." It has quickly climbed the usage charts on third-party AI developer platforms like OpenRouter, where it now ranks above Anthropic's models, while executives from cloud data platform Snowflake's CEO Sridhar Ramaswamy to venture capitalist Marc Andreessen have lauded its abilities. "We now have a Chinese open-weight model that is as good as the currently available models ​from OpenAI and Anthropic," said David Sacks, U.S. President Donald Trump's former AI czar, last week before Washington lifted curbs on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models on Tuesday. Those capabilities have put Z.ai's GLM-5.2 model ​at the heart of a growing debate about whether China is finally catching up to the U.S. in the AI race, as technology executives warn that Washington's unpredictable ⁠regulation of the industry risks hampering its lead in the frontier technology. "It is just a tick below Opus 4.8 (from Anthropic) and right up there with GPT 5.5 (from OpenAI)," Sacks said of GLM-5.2 on the All-In podcast, adding ​that "we cannot afford to do things that slow our companies down." The Anthropic curbs and the [delayed](https://www.reuters.com/legal/litigation/openai-defers-public-rollout-gpt56-us-seeks-early-access-frontier-ai-models-2026-06-26/) public rollout of OpenAI's latest GPT-5.6 model have fueled global demand for the Chinese model, some experts said. "The international developer community is increasingly ​aware that relying solely on proprietary, U.S.-based API models carries significant risk," said Brian Tse, founder and CEO of Concordia AI, a Beijing-based consultancy focused on AI safety. GLM-5.2's positive global reception also suggests increased interest in cheaper open-source development because businesses are getting stung by the rising and often unpredictable costs of using AI to complete tasks, as closed-source agentic AI tools consume more tokens, the units used to measure AI usage. Z.ai, also known as Zhipu AI, declined to comment....

LLMs.content: : [Consumer Ecosystems](/telecom-technology/60-consumer-ecosystems.html) ## BoGuan LLM and 5G-A boosts digital cultural tourism in Xi’an Details : Staff Writer : Category: [Consumer Ecosystems](/telecom-technology/60-consumer-ecosystems.html) : 28 views * [Asia-Pacific](/component/tags/tag/asia-pacific.html) * [China](/component/tags/tag/china.html) * [Huawei](/component/tags/tag/huawei.html) * [China Telecom](/component/tags/tag/china-telecom.html) * [5G-Advanced](/component/tags/tag/5g-advanced.html) * [Partner Content](/component/tags/tag/partner-content.html) * [LLMs](/component/tags/tag/llms.html) Less than a year after its commercial launch, the BoGuan multimodal large language model (LLM) designed for cultural tourism in China has entered broad application in Xi’an, and is generating real revenues and value for the tourism industry. As one of the oldest cities in China with a rich cultural heritage, Xi'an is one of the most popular international tourist destinations in China. The city has increasingly turned to digital technologies to enable visitors and scholars to access cultural relics and better engage with the region's rich history whilst also preserving its history. Shaanxi Culture Industry Investment Group (SCG) has been working with partners like Huawei, China Telecom Shaanxi, and China West Airport Group (CWAG) to promote digital and intelligent cultural tourism by leveraging advanced technologies like AI and 5G-A to create numerous new services for visitors. On the AI side of that initiative, SCG and Huawei unveiled the BoGuan LLM in September 2025, billing it as the world's first commercial multimodal LLM for cultural tourism, as well as China's first model dedicated specifically to preserve cultural heritage. **Inside BoGuan** The BoGuan LLM is built on intelligent computing infrastructure and a high-quality dataset that includes over 1.2 PB of data – including 31 million images, 4.4 million minutes of video footage, 2.18 million minutes of audio recordings, 510 3D models, and 960 million pieces of structured text. With that dataset, BoGuan can generate highly-accurate multimodal content, such as museum-quality content about cultural relics. This capability enables it to support creation of new digital relic presentations, digitalization and preservation of traditional craftsmanship, and creation of digital IP for intangible cultural heritage. BoGuan has also been used to develop a range of cultural tourism apps that unlock the business value of quality cultural tourism data. For example, BoGuan powers an AI travel companion agent that had been made available to over 4 million users as of March 2026. Visitors can directly talk with this agent on the GO-SHAANXI app to create and adjust their travel itineraries in Xi’an, and get real-time recommendations for performances at various attractions. Another app created using BoGuan is the Zhiying Camera mini programme – an AI-powered photography service that instantly integrates user photos with AI-generated scenes from history, allowing visitors to "time travel” to ancient times. The BoGuan LLM has also been used to create digital IP such as the popular cartoon character Tang Biaobiao, which was designed by integrating local cultural heritage elements with the stone carvings of the Six Steeds of Zhao Mausoleum. As a testament of Tang Biaobiao’s popularity, sales of related digital collectibles and creative products have exceeded CNY2 million (almost US$295,000). Meanwhile, SCG is also using BoGuan to integrate short drama production with cultural tourism and improve production efficiency and quality in Xi'an, which is also arenowned short drama hub. “Artificial intelligence is not simply a stack of technologies,” said Edric Chu, General Manager of Huawei's Shaanxi Rep Office. “It has become a key enabler that can activate thousands of years of cultural heritage, reshape travel experiences, and inject new momentum into the industry.” **Powered by 5G-A** All of thes...

LLMs.content: [Skip to content](#main-content) [LDS Let's Data ScienceLEARN • BUILD • STAY AHEAD](/) [Sign in](/login?redirect=%2Fnews%2Fbase44-launches-base1-to-improve-ui-generation-3728d950) Products & Toolsbase44base1vibe codingwix # Base44 launches Base1 to improve UI generation ||By [LDS Team](/editorial-standards) 6.9 Relevance Score Specialized models trained on product interaction data can change UX quality, latency, and cost trade-offs compared with generalist frontier models. According to Wix's corporate blog, Base44, acquired by Wix in June 2025, has launched a proprietary LLM called Base1 to power its vibe-coding app-creation workflow. TechCrunch and other outlets report the model was trained on a dataset derived from "tens of millions of real user interactions on the platform" (TechCrunch) and that Base44 will use reinforcement learning in ongoing tuning (Business Insider). Business Insider also quotes founder Maor Shlomo describing the motivation as avoiding the "generic" UI outputs common from frontier models. Reporting places the move in a broader pattern of startups building in-house models to reduce inference costs and increase product defensibility (TechCrunch, Aixploria). ### Editorial analysis Practitioners building UX- or design-sensitive applications should treat this as an example of the trade-offs between using frontier, generalist models and investing in a narrow, product-specific LLM. Specialized models can materially improve surface-level outputs (layout, visual style) while offering levers on latency and inference cost that are hard to obtain when routing every request to a third-party general model. **What happened**, Reported facts: According to Wix's corporate blog, Base44 launched a proprietary LLM called **`Base1`** to support its vibe-coding app-creation experience (Wix blog). TechCrunch reports the model was developed from a dataset generated by **"tens of millions of real user interactions on the platform"** and that the release is being rolled out to users (TechCrunch). Business Insider quotes Base44 founder Maor Shlomo saying part of the rationale was to combat generic UI outputs: "everybody feels like they're getting the same UI when they're coding with the general models" (Business Insider). Multiple outlets note that Base44 was acquired by Wix for **$80 million** in June 2025 (Business Insider, Aixploria). **Technical context and training approach**, Reported facts and sourcing: TechCrunch and Aixploria describe Base1 as a model fine-tuned and trained on Base44's product data, with reinforcement learning used to push the model toward more varied, distinctive UI generations (TechCrunch; Business Insider). Reporting indicates Base44 previously used external models such as OpenAI and Anthropic, and has added Base1 to a model selector that includes GPT-5.5 and Claude's Opus 4.8 (Business Insider). ### Industry context Public reporting frames this move within a wider pattern where AI products with sufficient user interactions and revenue consider owning model stacks to control costs and output quality (TechCrunch). Investors and analysts quoted in coverage, including Headline partner Jonathan Userovici, place data, distribution, and tech stack as the three ingredients of defensibility for AI startups (TechCrunch). **What this means for builders**, Editorial analysis: Teams that rely on generative models for UI or other high-dimension creative outputs should evaluate whether a narrow model trained on in-product signals will deliver better end-user variability and reduce repetitive "AI slop" outputs. The trade-off is nontrivial: owning a model adds operational complexity (training, RL loops, inference deployment), but reporting from Wix emphasizes lower inference cost and faster iteration cadence as key benefits (Wix blog). **Competitive and market signals**, Reported facts and synthesis: Coverage names competitors in the vibe-coding and app-creation space such as Lovable, Replit, and Cursor, and frames B...

LLMs.title: Your brand might have 10,000 media mentions and still not exist in AI search - The Drum
LLMs.titleZh: Your brand might have 10,000 media mentions and still not exist in AI search - The Drum
LLMs.content: back to thedrum.com [Technology](/technology) [Artificial Intelligence](/topics/artificial-intelligence) # Your brand might have 10,000 media mentions and still not exist in AI search #### By [Aya Elbaoudi,](/profile/onclusive/team/60b80e4e-6913-4d9d-abc2-7dd16aa72359) Field Marketing Assistant #### [Onclusive](/profile/onclusive) | [Member article](#) This content is produced by a member of The Drum Network, a paid-for membership club for CEOs and their agencies who want to share their expertise and grow their business. [Find out more](https://product.thedrum.com/the-drum-network) July 2, 2026 | 6 min read [Enquire with this member](https://form.jotform.com/242123363525348?company-slug=onclusive) [Listen to article](#) 4 min **The gap between earned media volume and AI visibility is the most important blind spot in communications right now. Here’s how to close it.** Something important has changed in how people discover brands, and most communications teams have not caught up yet. When a senior buyer wants to understand the leading platforms in your category, they’re increasingly not typing into Google. They are asking ChatGPT. They are querying Perplexity. They are using Gemini as a research assistant. And the answer they get back – a confident, curated paragraph naming three or four companies – shapes their thinking before they ever visit a website, read a press release or speak to a salesperson. The question is: is your brand in that paragraph? And if it is, what is the LLM saying about you? Most teams currently have no way to answer either question. ## **Why traditional monitoring cannot see this** Media monitoring tools are built to track what has been published. They capture mentions, measure sentiment, score reach and report coverage. They are very good at telling you what happened in the media yesterday. They cannot tell you what an AI engine decided to say about your brand today. AI-generated answers are not a reflection of your latest press coverage. They are shaped by a combination of factors: which sources the model has learned to trust, how prominently and consistently your brand appears across those sources, what narratives have attached to your name over time, and how you compare to competitors across all of those dimensions simultaneously. A brand with 10,000 media mentions can be invisible in AI search. A newer entrant with a fraction of that coverage can dominate AI answers in their category, because they have been consistently cited in the specific sources AI engines weight most heavily. This is the gap. And it‘s where brand influence is currently being won and lost, largely undetected. ## **What AI visibility actually measures** [Onclusive’s AI Visibility Playbook](https://hubs.li/Q04mNnxf0) introduces a framework built around five metrics that communications teams can track, report and act on. Visibility measures whether your brand appears in AI-generated answers at all. Share of Voice measures how often you appear relative to competitors across the same queries. Average Position tracks where in the answer your brand is mentioned, because being named third in a list of five is not the same as being the first name the model reaches for. LLM Choice captures whether AI engines are actively recommending your brand when someone asks which company to use. And Sentiment tracks how your brand is characterized in those answers, not just whether it appears. Together, these five metrics give communications leaders something they have never had before: a consistent, comparable, leadership-ready view of how AI search is representing their brand. ## **The four gaps you need to diagnose** [The playbook](https://hubs.li/Q04mNnxf0) identifies four types of gap that explain most underperformance in AI visibility. A presence gap means your brand is not appearing in AI answers for queries where it should be. This is the most basic problem and often the most fixable, because it points directly to source coverage deficiencies ...
